description: Poetry is a tool for dependency management and packaging in Python

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command

The working directory for the poetry command

subcommands.description: Adds a new dependency to pyproject.toml

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command
The group to add the dependency to
Add as a development dependency. (Deprecated) Use --group=dev instead
Extras to activate for the dependency
Python version for which the dependency must be installed
Platforms for which the dependency must be installed
Name of the source to use to install the package
Output the operations but do not execute anything (implicitly enables --verbose)
Do not perform operations (only update the lockfile)

The working directory for the poetry command

subcommands.description: Activates or creates a new virtualenv for the current project

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command

subcommands.description: Creates a basic pyproject.toml file in the current directory

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command
Package to require, with an optional version constraint, e.g. requests:^2.10.0 or requests=2.11.1
Package to require for development, with an optional version constraint, e.g. requests:^2.10.0 or requests=2.11.1

subcommands.description: Installs the project dependencies

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command
The optional dependency groups to include
The only dependency groups to include
Do not install the development dependencies. (Deprecated)
Synchronize the environment with the locked packages and the specified groups
Do not install the root package (the current project)
Do not install any directory path dependencies; useful to install dependencies without source code, e.g. for caching of Docker layers)
Output the operations but do not execute anything (implicitly enables --verbose)
Removes packages not present in the lock file. (Deprecated)
Extra sets of dependencies to install
Compile Python source files to bytecode. (This option has no effect if modern-installation is disabled because the old installer always compiles.)

subcommands.description: Locks the project dependencies

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command
Do not update locked versions, only refresh lock file
Check that the poetry.lock file corresponds to the current version of pyproject.toml

subcommands.description: Creates a new Python project at <path>

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command
Use the src layout for the project
Specify the readme file format. One of md (default) or rst

args.name: path
args.description: The path to create the project at

subcommands.description: Publishes a package to a remote repository

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command
The repository to publish the package to
The username to access the repository
The password to access the repository
Certificate authority to access the repository
Client certificate to access the repository
Perform all actions except upload the package
Ignore errors from files already existing in the repository

subcommands.description: The source namespace regroups sub commands to manage repository sources for a poetry project
Add source configuration for project

options.description: Do not ask any interactive question
The working directory for the poetry command
Set this source as the default (disable PyPI). A default source will also be the fallback source if you add other sources. (Deprecated, use --priority)
Set this source as secondary. (Deprecated, use --priority)
Set the priority of this source. One of: default, primary, secondary, supplemental, explicit. Defaults to primary

args.name: url
args.description: Source repository URL. Required, except for PyPI, for which it is not allowed
